channel.title: Endgame on Dyson Sphere Program Guide
channel.description: Recent content in Endgame on Dyson Sphere Program Guide

item.title: 1 Artificial Sun Replaces 125 Wind Turbines - DSP Endgame Power Loop Guide
item.description:
Critical photons and antimatter are the gateway to endgame power in DSP. The loop is confusing at first: photons come from the sphere, make antimatter, make fuel rods, power your planet. Here is exactly how the chain works, step by step.

TL;DR: The Endgame Power Loop

Ray Receivers capture Dyson Sphere power and output Critical Photons. Particle Colliders turn photons into Antimatter + Hydrogen. Assemblers combine Antimatter with other materials to make Deuterium Fuel Rods or Antimatter Fuel Rods. Artificial Suns burn these rods for massive power. The loop produces more power than it consumes - but only if designed correctly.

item.title: DSP Dyson Sphere Guide - Permanent First Layer Frame Strategy
item.description:
Building a Dyson Sphere is the endgame milestone in DSP. But the first attempt often fails: you launch sails, they expire, and you get zero power.

Here is the exact sequence to build a permanent, self-sustaining first layer.

TL;DR: The 4-Step Sequence

Build rockets first, not sails. A common trap: players launch thousands of solar sails and watch them expire (they last 1800 seconds - 30 minutes). Permanent sphere requires frame nodes, which require rockets. Build a rocket factory BEFORE scaling your sail launcher.

item.title: DSP Quantum Chips & Proliferator: Advanced Production Guide
item.description:
Every factory has a ceiling. At some point you're running 40 assemblers on your Circuit Board chain and wondering if there's a better way. In DSP v0.10, there is: Proliferator.

It's a resource you produce, spray onto your production lines, and watch your output climb without adding a single new assembler.

This guide covers the Quantum Chip production chain and explains the Proliferator system — what it is, how to produce it, what it does, and how to use it without turning your factory into a confusing mess of spray towers.

item.title: DSP Solar Sails & Ray Receivers: Complete Endgame Power Guide
item.description:
You've got a handful of coal power plants. Maybe you've built a wind farm or twelve. You're researching toward the stars — literally — and then it hits you: the Dyson sphere isn't just the goal, it's the power solution. To get there, you need Solar Sails and Ray Receivers working in tandem.

One launches into orbit and reflects stellar radiation. The other catches that energy and converts it into usable power for your planet.
